questions.question: Who is known as the Father of the Nation in India?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi is known as the Father of the Nation. He led India's freedom movement using non-violence and truth.
questions.funFact: Gandhi's birthday, October 2nd, is celebrated as the International Day of Non-Violence worldwide!

questions.question: Who was the first Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru became the first Prime Minister of India on August 15, 1947, when India gained independence.
questions.funFact: Nehru loved children so much that his birthday, November 14th, is celebrated as Children's Day in India!

questions.question: Which Indian cricketer is called the 'God of Cricket'?
questions.explanation: Sachin Tendulkar is called the God of Cricket. He scored more runs in international cricket than anyone else in history.
questions.funFact: Sachin started playing cricket when he was just 11 years old and played his first international match at age 16!

questions.question: Who wrote the book 'Wings of Fire'?
questions.explanation: APJ Abdul Kalam wrote 'Wings of Fire.' He was a great scientist who later became the President of India.
questions.funFact: Kalam loved playing a musical instrument called the veena and enjoyed spending time with children!

questions.question: Which kind lady helped sick and poor people in Kolkata and won the Nobel Peace Prize?
questions.explanation: Mother Teresa devoted her life to caring for the sick, poor, and homeless in Kolkata. She won the Nobel Peace Prize in 1979.
questions.funFact: Mother Teresa came to India from a small country called North Macedonia when she was just 18 years old!

questions.question: What did Mahatma Gandhi teach people to use instead of fighting?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi believed in non-violence, which he called 'ahimsa.' He showed the world that peace is stronger than fighting.
questions.funFact: Gandhi used a simple spinning wheel called a 'charkha' to make his own clothes as a symbol of self-reliance!

questions.question: APJ Abdul Kalam is known as the 'Missile Man of India.' What was his job before becoming President?
questions.explanation: APJ Abdul Kalam was a brilliant scientist who helped India build rockets and missiles before becoming the 11th President of India.
questions.funFact: As a child, Kalam used to sell newspapers to help his family earn money!

questions.question: What sport is Sachin Tendulkar famous for?
questions.explanation: Sachin Tendulkar is famous for playing cricket. He is considered one of the greatest batsmen in cricket history.
questions.funFact: Sachin hit 100 centuries (100 runs in one match) in international cricket — no one else has done that!

questions.question: Which famous Indian leader always wore a white cap called the 'Gandhi topi'?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru was known for wearing his signature white cap, which became known as the 'Nehru cap.'
questions.funFact: Nehru also always wore a fresh red rose on his jacket every single day!

questions.question: Who is called the 'Nightingale of India' because of her beautiful poetry?
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu was called the Nightingale of India for her beautiful poems. She was also a brave freedom fighter.
questions.funFact: Sarojini Naidu wrote her first poem when she was just 12 years old!

questions.question: Who is known as the 'Iron Man of India' for uniting all the states?
questions.explanation: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel united over 500 princely states into one India after independence. That is why he is called the Iron Man of India.
questions.funFact: The Statue of Unity in Gujarat, the world's tallest statue at 182 meters, was built in his honour!

questions.question: Which Indian singer was called the 'Melody Queen' and sang thousands of songs?
questions.explanation: Lata Mangeshkar was called the Melody Queen of India. She sang songs in over 36 languages during her amazing career.
questions.funFact: Lata Mangeshkar recorded more songs than almost any other singer in the world — over 25,000 songs!

questions.question: What did Jawaharlal Nehru call the children of India?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru lovingly called children the 'little stars' and believed they were the future of the country.
questions.funFact: Nehru wrote a famous book called 'Letters from a Father to His Daughter' for his daughter Indira when she was just 10!

questions.question: Which brave Indian queen fought against the British on a horse?
questions.explanation: Rani Lakshmibai of Jhansi was a brave queen who fought against the British in 1857. She is a symbol of courage in India.
questions.funFact: Rani Lakshmibai was trained in horse riding, sword fighting, and archery as a child — she could ride a horse with a sword in each hand!

questions.question: What was Mahatma Gandhi's full name?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi's full name was Mohandas Karamchand Gandhi. 'Mahatma' is a title meaning 'Great Soul.'
questions.funFact: Gandhi studied law in London, England, and once tried to learn to dance and play the violin there!

questions.question: Which Indian athlete won a gold medal in javelin throw at the Olympics?
questions.explanation: Neeraj Chopra won the gold medal in javelin throw at the 2020 Tokyo Olympics, making all of India proud.
questions.funFact: Neeraj started practicing javelin throw when he was just 13 years old at a stadium near his village in Haryana!

questions.question: Who was the first Indian to go to space?
questions.explanation: Rakesh Sharma became the first Indian to travel to space in 1984. He spent 8 days aboard a space station.
questions.funFact: When the Prime Minister asked Rakesh Sharma how India looked from space, he replied 'Saare Jahan Se Accha' — the best in the world!

questions.question: What did Mother Teresa do to help people?
options: Cared for the sick and poor
questions.explanation: Mother Teresa spent her life caring for the sick, poor, and dying people. She started the Missionaries of Charity in Kolkata.
questions.funFact: Mother Teresa was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize in 1979 and she wore a simple white sari with blue borders every day!

questions.question: Which Indian leader said 'Do or Die' during the freedom movement?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi gave the famous slogan 'Do or Die' during the Quit India Movement in 1942 to inspire people to fight for freedom.
questions.funFact: Gandhi also led the famous Salt March in 1930, walking 385 kilometres to the sea to make salt and protest unfair British laws!

questions.question: Which Indian badminton player won a silver medal at the Olympics?
questions.explanation: PV Sindhu won a silver medal at the 2016 Rio Olympics and a bronze at the 2020 Tokyo Olympics in badminton.
questions.funFact: Sindhu used to travel over 50 kilometres every day from her home to her training centre as a young girl!

questions.question: Subhash Chandra Bose founded which famous army to fight for India's freedom?
questions.explanation: Subhash Chandra Bose founded the Indian National Army (also called Azad Hind Fauj) to fight for India's independence from British rule.
questions.funFact: Bose's famous slogan was 'Give me blood, and I shall give you freedom!' He was also called 'Netaji' meaning respected leader!

questions.question: At what age was Bhagat Singh when he sacrificed his life for India's freedom?
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh was just 23 years old when he was executed by the British on March 23, 1931. He is one of India's youngest freedom fighters.
questions.funFact: Bhagat Singh loved reading and had a huge collection of books — he reportedly read over 300 books during his time in jail!

questions.question: Which Indian scientist won the Nobel Prize in Physics for his work on how light behaves?
questions.explanation: CV Raman won the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1930 for the 'Raman Effect,' which explains how light changes when it passes through different materials.
questions.funFact: February 28th is celebrated as National Science Day in India to honour CV Raman's discovery of the Raman Effect!

questions.question: Who is known as the father of the Indian nuclear programme?
questions.explanation: Homi J. Bhabha is called the father of the Indian nuclear programme. He set up India's atomic energy research and built the foundation for nuclear science in India.
questions.funFact: Bhabha was also a talented painter and loved classical music — he believed science and art go hand in hand!

questions.question: Mary Kom is a champion in which sport?
questions.explanation: Mary Kom is one of the greatest female boxers in the world. She has won six World Boxing Championship gold medals.
questions.funFact: Mary Kom started boxing secretly because her father, a wrestler, was worried she might get hurt!

questions.question: Which Indian poet wrote the national anthem 'Jana Gana Mana'?
questions.explanation: Rabindranath Tagore wrote 'Jana Gana Mana,' which became India's national anthem. He was a poet, writer, and artist.
questions.funFact: Tagore was the first non-European to win the Nobel Prize in Literature in 1913 for his collection of poems called 'Gitanjali'!

questions.question: What was Subhash Chandra Bose's famous greeting to his soldiers?
questions.explanation: Subhash Chandra Bose popularised the greeting 'Jai Hind,' meaning 'Victory to India.' It became a patriotic slogan used across the country.
questions.funFact: Bose escaped British house arrest in India in 1941 by disguising himself and secretly travelling through Afghanistan to reach Germany!

questions.question: Which Indian woman was the first female teacher in India?
questions.explanation: Savitribai Phule became India's first female teacher in 1848. Along with her husband Jyotirao Phule, she opened schools for girls when girls were not allowed to study.
questions.funFact: People used to throw stones and mud at Savitribai on her way to school, but she bravely carried an extra sari and kept going!

questions.question: How many World Boxing Championship gold medals has Mary Kom won?
questions.explanation: Mary Kom has won six World Boxing Championship gold medals, making her one of the most successful boxers in history.
questions.funFact: A Bollywood movie called 'Mary Kom' was made about her inspiring life, and she is also a member of the Rajya Sabha!

questions.question: What was the name of the movement where Gandhi asked people to make their own salt?
questions.explanation: The Salt March of 1930, also called the Dandi March, was when Gandhi walked 385 km to the sea to make salt and protest the unfair British salt tax.
questions.funFact: Thousands of people joined Gandhi during the march, and by the time he reached Dandi, the group had grown to tens of thousands!

questions.question: Which Indian leader said the famous words 'Tryst with Destiny' on the night India became independent?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru delivered the 'Tryst with Destiny' speech at midnight on August 14-15, 1947, when India gained independence.
questions.funFact: While Nehru gave his speech in Delhi, Mahatma Gandhi was in Kolkata and spent that night fasting and praying for peace!

questions.question: PV Sindhu became only the second Indian to win an individual Olympic medal in which sport?
questions.explanation: PV Sindhu won a silver medal in badminton at the 2016 Rio Olympics, becoming one of India's most celebrated athletes.
questions.funFact: Sindhu's full name is Pusarla Venkata Sindhu, and both her parents were national-level volleyball players!

questions.question: Which Indian freedom fighter threw a bomb in the Central Legislative Assembly to protest against unjust laws?
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh and Batukeshwar Dutt threw smoke bombs in the Central Legislative Assembly in 1929 to protest unjust British laws. They did not try to hurt anyone.
questions.funFact: Bhagat Singh purposely did not run after throwing the bombs — he wanted to be arrested so he could make his voice heard in court!

questions.question: Neeraj Chopra won India's first Olympic gold medal in which athletics event?
questions.explanation: Neeraj Chopra won gold in the javelin throw at the 2020 Tokyo Olympics with a throw of 87.58 metres. It was India's first Olympic gold in athletics.
questions.funFact: As a child, Neeraj was overweight, and his family enrolled him in sports to help him get fit — that is how he discovered javelin throw!

questions.question: Which Indian woman wrote the song 'Vande Mataram' that inspired freedom fighters?
options: No woman wrote it — Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay wrote it
questions.explanation: Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay wrote 'Vande Mataram' in his novel Anandamath in 1882. It became a rallying cry during the freedom movement.
questions.funFact: The song 'Vande Mataram' means 'I bow to thee, Mother' and refers to India as a mother goddess!

questions.question: Which famous Indian queen is remembered for her bravery in the Revolt of 1857?
questions.explanation: Rani Lakshmibai of Jhansi fought bravely against the British during the Revolt of 1857. She died fighting on the battlefield at just 29 years old.
questions.funFact: Rani Lakshmibai is said to have ridden into battle with her adopted son tied to her back so she could fight with both hands!

questions.question: Which scientist is known as the father of the Indian space programme?
questions.explanation: Vikram Sarabhai is the father of the Indian space programme. He founded the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) and made India a space-faring nation.
questions.funFact: India's first rocket parts were transported on a bicycle and a bullock cart to the launch site in Thumba, Kerala!

questions.question: Rabindranath Tagore won the Nobel Prize for which famous book of poems?
questions.explanation: Rabindranath Tagore won the Nobel Prize in Literature in 1913 for 'Gitanjali,' a collection of deeply spiritual poems.
questions.funFact: Tagore did not like going to school as a child and was mostly educated at home — he later started his own school called Shantiniketan!

questions.question: BR Ambedkar is remembered as the chief architect of which important document?
questions.explanation: Dr. BR Ambedkar was the chairman of the drafting committee that wrote the Constitution of India, which is the supreme law of the country.
questions.funFact: Ambedkar had the most degrees of any Indian leader of his time — he earned doctorates from both Columbia University and the London School of Economics!

questions.question: Swami Vivekananda became world-famous after his speech at which event in 1893?
questions.explanation: Swami Vivekananda delivered his legendary speech at the World Parliament of Religions in Chicago in 1893, beginning with 'Sisters and Brothers of America.'
questions.funFact: When Vivekananda said 'Sisters and Brothers of America,' the audience of 7,000 people gave him a standing ovation that lasted for two full minutes!

questions.question: What social evil did BR Ambedkar fight against throughout his life?
questions.explanation: BR Ambedkar fought against caste discrimination and untouchability. He dedicated his life to securing equal rights for all people regardless of their caste.
questions.funFact: As a child, Ambedkar was made to sit outside his classroom because of his caste — this experience drove him to fight for equality for millions of people!

questions.question: Which Indian scientist's theoretical work on nuclear physics led to an important particle being named after him?
questions.explanation: Satyendra Nath Bose's work in quantum physics led to the 'boson' particle being named after him. He collaborated with Albert Einstein on groundbreaking research.
questions.funFact: The famous Higgs Boson particle, often called the 'God Particle,' gets the 'boson' part of its name from Satyendra Nath Bose!

questions.question: What did Savitribai Phule and Jyotirao Phule establish in 1848 that was revolutionary for its time?
options: One of India's first schools for girls
questions.explanation: Savitribai and Jyotirao Phule opened one of India's first schools for girls in Pune in 1848, at a time when educating girls was strongly opposed by society.
questions.funFact: Savitribai Phule also opened a care centre for pregnant widows and worked to prevent infanticide — she adopted a widow's son and educated him to become a doctor!

questions.question: Vikram Sarabhai convinced the Indian government to invest in space research for what main purpose?
options: Using space technology for development and communication
questions.explanation: Vikram Sarabhai believed space technology should be used to solve real problems like communication, weather forecasting, and education in rural India.
questions.funFact: Sarabhai came from a famous industrialist family and could have lived a comfortable life, but he chose to dedicate himself to science and nation-building!

questions.question: What was Swami Vivekananda's original name before he became a monk?
questions.explanation: Swami Vivekananda was born as Narendranath Datta. He became a monk and disciple of Ramakrishna Paramahamsa before taking the name Vivekananda.
questions.funFact: Vivekananda's birthday, January 12th, is celebrated as National Youth Day in India because he was a great inspiration to young people!

questions.question: CV Raman's Nobel Prize-winning discovery, the Raman Effect, explains what phenomenon?
options: How light scatters when passing through a transparent material
questions.explanation: The Raman Effect explains that when light passes through a transparent material, some of the light changes its wavelength. This discovery has applications in chemistry, physics, and medicine.
questions.funFact: CV Raman was so confident in his discovery that he booked tickets to the Nobel Prize ceremony in Sweden even before the winner was officially announced!

questions.question: Which Indian reformer established the Ramakrishna Mission to serve people and spread spiritual knowledge?
questions.explanation: Swami Vivekananda founded the Ramakrishna Mission in 1897 in honour of his guru Ramakrishna Paramahamsa. It works in education, healthcare, and disaster relief across India.
questions.funFact: Vivekananda walked across almost the entire length of India on foot to understand the lives of ordinary people before going to America!

questions.question: Sardar Patel is compared to which famous European leader for uniting many small states into one nation?
questions.explanation: Sardar Patel is often compared to Otto von Bismarck of Germany because both leaders united many small states into a single powerful nation through diplomacy and determination.
questions.funFact: Patel convinced 562 princely states to join India, mostly through peaceful negotiation — only a handful required any show of force!

questions.question: Which Indian Nobel laureate's work at the Raman Research Institute continues to influence modern science?
questions.explanation: CV Raman founded the Raman Research Institute in Bangalore in 1948. His discovery of the Raman Effect is still used today in fields ranging from medicine to environmental science.
questions.funFact: Raman resigned from his government job to pursue science full-time with a much lower salary — his passion for discovery was more important than money!

questions.question: What was unique about BR Ambedkar's personal journey that made his achievements even more remarkable?
options: He was born into a royal family but gave up his wealth
He overcame caste discrimination to become one of India's greatest scholars and leaders
He was a freedom fighter who spent decades in prison
He was a foreign-born citizen who chose India as his home
questions.explanation: BR Ambedkar was born into an untouchable Dalit family and faced severe caste discrimination. Despite this, he earned multiple doctorates abroad and shaped modern India's Constitution and laws.
questions.funFact: Ambedkar's personal library had over 50,000 books, making it one of the largest private libraries in the world at that time!
